title: Iran Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia — 10 US Troops Wounded
detail: Iran launched a ballistic missile strike against Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, wounding at least 10 US service members (two seriously) and damaging refueling aircraft. The strike marks a significant escalation — the first confirmed Iranian direct strike on a major US air base in Saudi Arabia during the conflict. The attack targeted the base used by US aircraft for refueling operations supporting the Iran campaign. Two wounded were evacuated for emergency surgery. Pentagon confirmed the attack and the casualties; CENTCOM released a statement stating operations continue uninterrupted. The strike brought total US wounded to 150+ for the conflict.

title: IDF Strikes Arak Heavy Water Reactor and Yazd Yellowcake Plant
detail: US and Israeli airstrikes targeted the Arak heavy water research reactor complex and a yellowcake uranium production plant in Yazd province, per Iranian state media and corroborated by Western sources. The Arak reactor, which had been subject to the 2015 JCPOA modification requirements, was struck in an effort to permanently eliminate Iran's ability to produce plutonium. The Yazd yellowcake facility, which processes uranium ore into yellowcake for enrichment feedstock, was simultaneously struck. These strikes represent the deepest attack yet on Iran's nuclear fuel cycle infrastructure beyond the well-documented strikes on Natanz and Fordow. Iran's nuclear program is now assessed as having been set back by decades. Two of Iran's largest steel manufacturing facilities were also struck in the same wave, targeting economic infrastructure funding the IRGC.

title: Iranian Missile Hits Eshtaol Near Jerusalem — IDF Failed to Intercept
detail: An Iranian ballistic missile successfully struck the Jerusalem-area town of Eshtaol, leaving several people lightly injured. The Israeli Air Force failed to intercept the missile — the IAF opened an investigation into the interception failure. Rocket trails were separately seen above Netanya during a fresh barrage. The successful strike on Eshtaol — inside the greater Jerusalem metropolitan area — represents one of Iran's most significant penetrations of Israeli air defenses in the conflict. Iran's ability to continue launching strikes despite IDF/CENTCOM claims of near-total destruction of Iran's missile production capacity reflects the size of pre-war stockpiles. IDF Iron Dome and Arrow systems remained active across the country.

title: Houthis Launch Ballistic Missile at Israel — Beersheba Sirens
detail: Houthi forces in Yemen launched a ballistic missile toward Israel on March 28, triggering air raid sirens in Beersheba in southern Israel. The missile was tracked by Israeli air defense systems. The attack marks renewed Houthi solidarity missile launches in coordination with Iranian retaliatory operations. The Houthis had reduced their strike tempo since the US counter-campaign in Yemen earlier in the conflict, but have resumed attacks as Iran continues to face US-Israeli airstrikes. Israel's multi-layered air defense system was activated. No casualties were immediately confirmed from the Beersheba-area alert.

title: IDF Conducts Overnight Strikes on Beirut Southern Suburbs
detail: The IDF conducted overnight airstrikes on Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) and the Deir al-Zahrani area in south Lebanon. Lebanese journalists from Al Manar TV and Al Mayadeen were killed in an IDF strike in south Lebanon; Lebanese President Joseph Aoun condemned the strikes in a statement calling for international intervention. Lebanon's cumulative death toll from the conflict rose to 1,142+ including 130+ children and more than 1 million displaced. IDF is maintaining a declared 30km security zone to the Litani River and continues to conduct strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon and the Beirut periphery.

title: IRGC Establishes 'Tehran Toll Booth' — Charging Millions per Tanker Transit
detail: The IRGC has established a 'Tehran toll booth' system at the Strait of Hormuz, forcing tankers through a narrow IRGC-controlled passage and charging millions of dollars per vessel for safe transit. NBC News and USNI News reported that at least 26 ships have used the tolled passage since approximately March 13. Thailand announced a bilateral deal with Iran on March 28 allowing Thai oil vessels safe passage under this framework. China, India, Pakistan, and Russia-flagged tankers are the primary beneficiaries of selective IRGC-managed passage arrangements. Shipping transits are down 90–95% from pre-war levels: only 142 transits recorded March 1–25 versus 2,652 in the same period in 2025. Ship insurance costs have risen to 3.5–10% of vessel value.

title: Trump Warns Iran to 'Get Serious Soon'; Rubio Acknowledges Unclear Negotiating Partner
detail: President Trump warned Iran that negotiators 'better get serious soon, before it is too late,' signaling the April 6 deadline is firm. US Secretary of State Rubio acknowledged in a Friday statement that it remains unclear who will speak for Iran in any formal negotiations — a significant admission given the elimination of much of Iran's senior leadership. UK National Security Adviser Jonathan Powell secretly attended US-Iran nuclear negotiations in Geneva, per The Guardian — the first confirmed British presence in the talks. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf has been in contact with US envoys Witkoff and Kushner (per Axios), representing the Iranian side given the degraded state of Iran's executive branch. Iran formally responded to Trump's 15-point plan, calling it 'one-sided and unreasonable.'
